data.title: Logansport Financial Corp. reported net earnings of $413,000 in Q2 2025, up from $349,000 in 2024. Total assets reached $260.2 million, deposits $223.8 million. Dividends paid: $0.90/share in 1H 2025, same as in 2024.
data.description: Logansport Financial Corp., parent company of Logansport Savings Bank, reported net earnings of $413,000 for the quarter ended June 30, 2025, compared to $349,000 in 2024. Year-to-date earnings for 2025 were $790,000 versus $617,000 in 2024. Diluted earnings per share for the first half of 2025 were $1.45, up from $1.01 in 2024. Total assets as of June 30, 2025, were $260.2 million, an increase from $249.6 million in 2024. Total deposits were $223.8 million in 2025, compared to $211.7 million in 2024. The company paid $0.90 per share in dividends in the first half of 2025, unchanged from 2024. Forward-looking statements in the press release highlighted potential risks and uncertainties impacting future financial performance. Key financial data for June 30, 2025, and June 30, 2024, were also provided.

data.title: Intel's Q2 revenue exceeded expectations at $12.86B, but EPS missed at -10 cents per share. Key growth areas: Client Computing, Data Center and AI, All Other. Stock details on Benzinga.com.
data.description: Intel Corp (NASDAQ: INTC) released its second-quarter financial results, exceeding expectations. The company reported a revenue of $12.86 billion, surpassing analyst predictions of $11.91 billion. However, Intel reported an adjusted loss of 10 cents per share for the quarter, falling short of the estimated one cent per share in earnings. Despite this, the total revenue for the quarter remained flat compared to the previous year. The breakdown of revenue by category is as follows: Client Computing Group generated $7.9 billion, marking a 3% decline year-over-year. Data Center and AI brought in $3.9 billion, showing a 4% increase over the previous year. The All Other category recorded $1.1 billion in revenue, representing a substantial 20% growth year-over-year. Overall, Total Intel Products amounted to $11.8 billion. Additional details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Geospace Technologies to report Q3 and 9-month results on Aug 7, 2025; earnings call set for Aug 8 at 10 a.m. ET.
data.description: Houston-based Geospace, listed on NASDAQ as GEOS, is set to announce its third-quarter results for 2025 and nine-month performance on August 7, 2025. The company has scheduled a conference call for August 8, 2025, at 10 a.m. Eastern Time to discuss these results.

data.title: Google supported blockchain game in global campaign; My Lovely Planet 1st Web3 game featured on Google Play. Blockchain seen as technology layer, not just for money. Future is frictionless integration for platform adoption.
data.description: The CEO of My Lovely Planet, Clément Le Bras, notes that Web3 gaming is moving closer to mainstream acceptance, with platforms like Google Play starting to view blockchain as a seamless backend component rather than just a way to make money. This shift is seen as a significant step for blockchain-based games as My Lovely Planet recently became the first Web3 game featured in Google's global #WeArePlay campaign. Le Bras emphasizes that platforms now see blockchain as a technology layer and not just a revenue strategy. He envisions a future where the integration of blockchain is smooth and unobtrusive for users, providing added value as trust and engagement grow. The current stock price of Google parent company Alphabet can be checked for updates.

data.title: Fortis Inc. will discuss Q2 2025 results on August 1, with teleconference and webcast at 8:30 a.m. Key speakers included.
data.description: St. John's, Newfoundland and Labrador, July 24, 2025 - Fortis Inc. (TSX/NYSE: FTS) is set to announce its Q2 2025 financial results on August 1. A teleconference and webcast are scheduled for 8:30 a.m. (Eastern) on the same day. The discussion will involve David Hutchens, President and CEO, and Jocelyn Perry, EVP and CFO.

data.title: CAGW selected Missouri Governor Mike Kehoe as July 2025 Porker of the Month for diverting funds to KC sports stadiums.
data.description: The state of Missouri Governor, Mike Kehoe, has recently been dubbed the July 2025 Porker of the Month by Citizens Against Government Waste (CAGW). This title was given to Governor Kehoe due to his decision to allocate significant taxpayer funds towards NFL and MLB stadiums in Kansas City. Governor Kehoe, a Republican, has come under fire for this move and is facing criticism for his handling of taxpayer money.
